# Dependency Inference Task

## Objective

Edit the build file(s) to include all necessary dependency configurations so the project builds and tests pass.

## Task Information

- **Language**: python
- **Project**: inducer/cgen
- **Build Files to Edit**: `pyproject.toml`

## Environment

```json
{
  "SDK": "Python 3.8",
  "OS": "ubuntu-22.04"
}
```

## Instructions

1. Analyze the project structure in the repository to understand what external libraries are used
2. Review source code imports and usage to identify all required dependencies
3. Edit the build file(s) listed above to add correct dependency declarations
4. Include ALL dependencies needed for the project to build and run its tests

### Constraints

- Only edit the files listed in "Build Files to Edit"
- Limit edits to dependency-related configurations
- Do not modify source code files
I'll help you analyze the project and add the necessary dependencies to `pyproject.toml`. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and running the tests to see what's needed.

file.content:
[build-system]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
requires = [
    "setuptools>=63",
]

[project]
name = "cgen"
version = "2020.2"
description = "C/C++ source generation from an AST"
readme = "README.rst"
license = { text = "MIT" }
authors = [
    { name = "Andreas Kloeckner", email = "inform@tiker.net" },
]
requires-python = ">=3.8"
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    "Intended Audience :: Other Audience",
    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
    "Natural Language :: English",
    "Programming Language :: Python",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries",
    "Topic :: Utilities",
]
dependencies = []

[project.optional-dependencies]
test = [
    "pytest",
    "ruff",
]

[project.urls]
Documentation = "https://documen.tician.de/cgen"
Homepage = "https://github.com/inducer/cgen"

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
include = [
    "cgen*",
]

[tool.ruff]
preview = true

[tool.ruff.lint]
extend-select = [
    "B",   # flake8-bugbear
    "C",   # flake8-comprehensions
    "E",   # pycodestyle
    "F",   # pyflakes
    "G",   # flake8-logging-format
    "I",   # flake8-isort
    "N",   # pep8-naming
    "Q",   # flake8-quotes
    "RUF", # ruff
    "UP",  # pyupgrade
    "W",   # pycodestyle
]
extend-ignore = [
    "C90",   # McCabe complexity
    "E226",  # missing whitespace around arithmetic operator
    "E241",  # multiple spaces after comma
    "E242",  # tab after comma
    "E402",  # module level import not at the top of file
    "UP031", # use f-strings instead of %
    "UP032", # use f-strings instead of .format
]

[tool.ruff.lint.flake8-quotes]
docstring-quotes = "double"
inline-quotes = "double"
multiline-quotes = "double"

[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
combine-as-imports = true
known-first-party = [ "pytools" ]
known-local-folder = [ "cgen" ]
lines-after-imports = 2

[build-system]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
requires = [
    "setuptools>=63",
]

[project]
name = "cgen"
version = "2020.2"
description = "C/C++ source generation from an AST"
readme = "README.rst"
license = { text = "MIT" }
authors = [
    { name = "Andreas Kloeckner", email = "inform@tiker.net" },
]
requires-python = ">=3.8"
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    "Intended Audience :: Other Audience",
    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
    "Natural Language :: English",
    "Programming Language :: Python",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries",
    "Topic :: Utilities",
]
dependencies = []

[project.optional-dependencies]
test = [
    "pytest",
    "ruff",
]

[project.urls]
Documentation = "https://documen.tician.de/cgen"
Homepage = "https://github.com/inducer/cgen"

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
include = [
    "cgen*",
]

[tool.ruff]
preview = true

[tool.ruff.lint]
extend-select = [
    "B",   # flake8-bugbear
    "C",   # flake8-comprehensions
    "E",   # pycodestyle
    "F",   # pyflakes
    "G",   # flake8-logging-format
    "I",   # flake8-isort
    "N",   # pep8-naming
    "Q",   # flake8-quotes
    "RUF", # ruff
    "UP",  # pyupgrade
    "W",   # pycodestyle
]
extend-ignore = [
    "C90",   # McCabe complexity
    "E226",  # missing whitespace around arithmetic operator
    "E241",  # multiple spaces after comma
    "E242",  # tab after comma
    "E402",  # module level import not at the top of file
    "UP031", # use f-strings instead of %
    "UP032", # use f-strings instead of .format
]

[tool.ruff.lint.flake8-quotes]
docstring-quotes = "double"
inline-quotes = "double"
multiline-quotes = "double"

[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
combine-as-imports = true
known-first-party = [ "pytools" ]
known-local-folder = [ "cgen" ]
lines-after-imports = 2

1→"""Generator for C/C++."""
     2→
     3→
     4→__copyright__ = "Copyright (C) 2008 Andreas Kloeckner"
     5→
     6→__license__ = """
     7→Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy
     8→of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal
     9→in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights
    10→to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell
    11→copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is
    12→furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:
    13→
    14→The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in
    15→all copies or substantial portions of the Software.
    16→
    17→THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR
    18→IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY,
    19→FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
    20→AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER
    21→LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM,
    22→OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN
    23→THE SOFTWARE.
    24→"""
    25→
    26→from typing import Any
    27→
    28→import numpy
    29→
    30→from pytools import memoize, memoize_method
    31→
    32→
    33→try:
    34→    import pycuda._pvt_struct as _struct
    35→except ImportError:
    36→    import struct as _struct
    37→
    38→
    39→@memoize
    40→def is_long_64_bit():
    41→    return _struct.calcsize("l") == 8
    42→
    43→
    44→def dtype_to_ctype(dtype):
    45→    if dtype is None:
    46→        raise ValueError("dtype may not be None")
    47→
    48→    dtype = numpy.dtype(dtype)
    49→    if dtype == numpy.int64:
    50→        if is_long_64_bit():
    51→            return "long"
    52→        else:
    53→            return "long long"
    54→    elif dtype == numpy.uint64:
    55→        if is_long_64_bit():
    56→            return "unsigned long"
    57→        else:
    58→            return "unsigned long long"
    59→    elif dtype == numpy.int32:
    60→        return "int"
    61→    elif dtype == numpy.uint32:
    62→        return "unsigned int"
    63→    elif dtype == numpy.int16:
    64→        return "short int"
    65→    elif dtype == numpy.uint16:
    66→        return "short unsigned int"
    67→    elif dtype == numpy.int8:
    68→        return "signed char"
    69→    elif dtype == numpy.uint8:
    70→        return "unsigned char"
    71→    elif dtype == numpy.float32:
    72→        return "float"
    73→    elif dtype == numpy.float64:
    74→        return "double"
    75→    elif dtype == numpy.complex64:
    76→        return "std::complex<float>"
    77→    elif dtype == numpy.complex128:
    78→        return "std::complex<double>"
    79→    elif dtype == numpy.void:
    80→        return "void"
    81→    else:
    82→        raise ValueError(f"unable to map dtype '{dtype}'")
    83→
    84→
    85→class Generable:
    86→    def __str__(self):
    87→        """Return a single string (possibly containing newlines) representing
    88→        this code construct."""
    89→        return "\n".join(line.rstrip() for line in self.generate())
    90→
    91→    def generate(self, with_semicolon=True):
    92→        """Generate (i.e. yield) the lines making up this code construct."""
    93→
    94→        raise NotImplementedError
    95→
    96→
    97→# {{{ declarators
    98→
    99→class Declarator(Generable):
   100→    def generate(self, with_semicolon=True):
   101→        tp_lines, tp_decl = self.get_decl_pair()
   102→        tp_lines = list(tp_lines)
   103→        yield from tp_lines[:-1]
   104→        sc = ";"
   105→        if not with_semicolon:
   106→            sc = ""
   107→        if tp_decl is None:
   108→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]}{sc}"
   109→        else:
   110→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]} {tp_decl}{sc}"
   111→
   112→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   113→        """Return a tuple ``(type_lines, rhs)``.
   114→
   115→        *type_lines* is a non-empty list of lines (most often just a
   116→        single one) describing the type of this declarator. *rhs* is the right-
   117→        hand side that actually contains the function/array/constness notation
   118→        making up the bulk of the declarator syntax.
   119→        """
   120→        raise NotImplementedError
   121→
   122→    def inline(self, with_semicolon=True):
   123→        """Return the declarator as a single line."""
   124→        tp_lines, tp_decl = self.get_decl_pair()
   125→        tp_lines = " ".join(tp_lines)
   126→        if tp_decl is None:
   127→            return tp_lines
   128→        else:
   129→            return f"{tp_lines} {tp_decl}"
   130→
   131→
   132→class POD(Declarator):
   133→    """A simple declarator: The type is given as a :class:`numpy.dtype`
   134→    and the *name* is given as a string.
   135→    """
   136→
   137→    def __init__(self, dtype, name):
   138→        self.dtype = numpy.dtype(dtype)
   139→        self.name = name
   140→
   141→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   142→        return [dtype_to_ctype(self.dtype)], self.name
   143→
   144→    def struct_maker_code(self, name):
   145→        return name
   146→
   147→    def struct_format(self):
   148→        return self.dtype.char
   149→
   150→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   151→        return _struct.calcsize(self.struct_format())
   152→
   153→    def default_value(self):
   154→        return 0
   155→
   156→    mapper_method = "map_pod"

   181→class NestedDeclarator(Declarator):
   182→    def __init__(self, subdecl):
   183→        self.subdecl = subdecl
   184→
   185→    @property
   186→    def name(self):
   187→        return self.subdecl.name
   188→
   189→    def struct_format(self):
   190→        return self.subdecl.struct_format()
   191→
   192→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   193→        return self.subdecl.alignment_requirement()
   194→
   195→    def struct_maker_code(self, data):
   196→        return self.subdecl.struct_maker_code(data)
   197→
   198→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   199→        return self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   200→
   201→
   202→class DeclSpecifier(NestedDeclarator):
   203→    def __init__(self, subdecl, spec, sep=" "):
   204→        NestedDeclarator.__init__(self, subdecl)
   205→        self.spec = spec
   206→        self.sep = sep
   207→
   208→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   209→        def add_spec(sub_it):
   210→            it = iter(sub_it)
   211→            try:
   212→                yield f"{self.spec}{self.sep}{next(it)}"
   213→            except StopIteration:
   214→                pass
   215→
   216→            yield from it
   217→
   218→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   219→        return add_spec(sub_tp), sub_decl
   220→
   221→

   223→    def __init__(self, namespace, subdecl):
   224→        DeclSpecifier.__init__(self, subdecl, namespace, "::")

   226→    mapper_method = "map_namespace_qualifier"

   288→class AlignedAttribute(NestedDeclarator):

   290→    Assigns an alignment for a definition of a type or an array.

   296→    def get_decl_pair(self):

   303→class AlignValueAttribute(NestedDeclarator):
   304→    """
   305→    Assigns an alignment for value of a pointer.
   306→
   307→    This is used for pointers where the user guarantees to the compiler that the
   308→    value of the pointer has the alignment stated. :class:`AlignedAttribute` on
   309→    the other hand tells the compiler to declare the type or the array with the
   310→    given alignment and cannot be used for telling the compiler that an existing
   311→    pointer has a certain alignment guarantee.
   312→    This attribute is currently supported by clang [1] and Intel [2] and is ignored
   313→    by gcc.
   314→
   315→    [1]: https://reviews.llvm.org/D4635
   316→    [2]: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/develop/documentation/oneapi-dpcpp-cpp-compiler-dev-guide-and-reference/top/compiler-reference/attributes/align-value.html
   317→    """
   318→    def __init__(self, align_bytes, subdecl):
   319→        super().__init__(subdecl)
   320→        self.align_bytes = align_bytes
   321→
   322→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   323→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   324→        return (sub_tp,
   325→            f"{sub_decl} __attribute__ ((align_value ({self.align_bytes})))")
   326→
   327→    mapper_method = "map_align_value"

   366→class ArrayOf(NestedDeclarator):
   367→    def __init__(self, subdecl, count=None):
   368→        NestedDeclarator.__init__(self, subdecl)
   369→        self.count = count
   370→
   371→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   372→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   373→        if self.count is None:
   374→            count_str = ""
   375→        else:
   376→            count_str = str(self.count)
   377→        return sub_tp, (f"{sub_decl}[{count_str}]")
   378→
   379→    def struct_maker_code(self, name):
   380→        return ", ".join(f"{name}[{i}]" for i in range(self.count))
   381→
   382→    def struct_format(self):
   383→        if self.count is None:
   384→            return "P"
   385→        else:
   386→            return f"{self.count}{self.subdecl.struct_format()}"
   387→
   388→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   389→        return self.subdecl.alignment_requirement()
   390→
   391→    def default_value(self):
   392→        return self.count*[self.subdecl.default_value()]
   393→
   394→    mapper_method = "map_array_of"

   397→class FunctionDeclaration(NestedDeclarator):

   404→
   405→        return sub_tp, ("{}({})".format(
   406→            sub_decl,
   407→            ", ".join(ad.inline() for ad in self.arg_decls)))

   409→    def struct_maker_code(self, data):
   410→        raise RuntimeError("function pointers can't be put into structs")

   415→    mapper_method = "map_function_declaration"

   420→# {{{ struct-like
   421→
   422→class Struct(Declarator):
   423→    """A structure declarator."""
   424→
   425→    def __init__(self, tpname, fields, declname=None, pad_bytes=0):
   426→        """Initialize the structure declarator.
   427→        *tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
   428→        name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
   429→        padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
   430→        *fields* is a list of :class:`Declarator` instances.
   431→        """
   432→        self.tpname = tpname
   433→        self.fields = fields
   434→        self.declname = declname
   435→        self.pad_bytes = pad_bytes
   436→
   437→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   438→        def get_tp():
   439→            if self.tpname is not None:
   440→                yield f"struct {self.tpname}"
   441→            else:
   442→                yield "struct"
   443→            yield "{"
   444→            for f in self.fields:
   445→                for f_line in f.generate():
   446→                    yield f"  {f_line}"
   447→            if self.pad_bytes:
   448→                yield f"  unsigned char _cgen_pad[{self.pad_bytes}];"
   449→            yield "} " + self.struct_attributes()
   450→        return get_tp(), self.declname
   451→
   452→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   453→        return max(f.alignment_requirement() for f in self.fields)
   454→
   455→    def struct_attributes(self):
   456→        return ""
   457→
   458→    mapper_method = "map_struct"
   459→
   460→
   461→class GenerableStruct(Struct):
   462→    def __init__(self, tpname, fields, declname=None,
   463→            align_bytes=None, aligned_prime_to=None):
   464→        """Initialize a structure declarator.
   465→        *tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
   466→        name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
   467→        padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
   468→        *fields* is a list of :class:`Declarator` instances.
   469→
   470→        *align_bytes* is an integer that causes the structure to be
   471→        padded to an integer multiple of itself.
   472→        *aligned_prime_to* is a list of integers.
   473→        If the resulting structure's size
   474→        is ``s``, then ``s//align_bytes`` will be made prime to all
   475→        numbers in *aligned_prime_to*. (Sounds obscure? It's needed
   476→        for avoiding bank conflicts in CUDA programming.)
   477→        """
   478→        if aligned_prime_to is None:
   479→            aligned_prime_to = []
   480→
   481→        format = "".join(f.struct_format() for f in fields)
   482→        bytes = _struct.calcsize(format)
   483→
   484→        natural_align_bytes = max(f.alignment_requirement() for f in fields)
   485→        if align_bytes is None:
   486→            align_bytes = natural_align_bytes
   487→        elif align_bytes < natural_align_bytes:
   488→            from warnings import warn
   489→            warn("requested struct alignment smaller than natural alignment",
   490→                 stacklevel=2)
   491→
   492→        self.align_bytes = align_bytes
   493→
   494→        padded_bytes = ((bytes + align_bytes - 1) // align_bytes) * align_bytes
   495→
   496→        def satisfies_primality(n):
   497→            import math
   498→            for p in aligned_prime_to:
   499→                if math.gcd(n, p) != 1:
   500→                    return False
   501→            return True
   502→
   503→        while not satisfies_primality(padded_bytes // align_bytes):
   504→            padded_bytes += align_bytes
   505→
   506→        Struct.__init__(self, tpname, fields, declname, padded_bytes - bytes)
   507→
   508→        if self.pad_bytes:
   509→            self.format = format + f"{self.pad_bytes}x"
   510→            self.bytes = padded_bytes
   511→        else:
   512→            self.format = format
   513→            self.bytes = bytes
   514→
   515→        assert _struct.calcsize(self.format) == self.bytes
   516→
   517→    # until nvcc bug is fixed
   518→    # def struct_attributes(self):
   519→    #     return "__attribute__ ((packed))"
   520→
   521→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   522→        return self.align_bytes
   523→
   524→    def make(self, **kwargs):
   525→        """Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
   526→        :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
   527→        in *kwargs*.
   528→        """
   529→        return self._maker()(_struct.pack, **kwargs)
   530→
   531→    def make_with_defaults(self, **kwargs):
   532→        """Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
   533→        :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
   534→        in *kwargs*.
   535→
   536→        Unlike :meth:`make`, not all members have to occur in *kwargs*.
   537→        """
   538→        return self._maker(with_defaults=True)(_struct.pack, **kwargs)
   539→
   540→    @memoize_method
   541→    def _maker(self, with_defaults=False):
   542→        def format_arg(f):
   543→            if with_defaults:
   544→                return f"{f.name}={f.default_value()!r}"
   545→            else:
   546→                return f.name
   547→
   548→        code = "lambda pack, {}: pack({}, {})".format(
   549→                ", ".join(format_arg(f) for f in self.fields),
   550→                repr(self.struct_format()),
   551→                ", ".join(f.struct_maker_code(f.name) for f in self.fields))
   552→        return eval(code)
   553→
   554→    def struct_format(self):
   555→        """Return the format of the struct as digested by the :mod:`struct`
   556→        module.
   557→        """
   558→        return self.format
   559→
   560→    def __len__(self):
   561→        """Return the number of bytes occupied by this struct."""
   562→        return self.bytes
   563→
   564→    mapper_method = "map_generable_struct"
   565→
   566→
   567→class Enum(Generable):
   568→    """An enum-like class for Python that can generate an equivalent C-level
   569→    declaration. Does not work within the usual "declarator" framework
   570→    because it uses macros to define values, and a separate typedef to define the
   571→    value type.
   572→
   573→    .. versionadded:: 2013.2
   574→
   575→    To use, derive from this class, and define the following things:
   576→
   577→    .. attribute:: c_name
   578→    .. attribute:: dtype
   579→    .. attribute:: c_value_prefix
   580→
   581→        A (usually upper-case) prefix to be used as a prefix to the names defined
   582→        on the Python side.
   583→    .. attribute:: VALUE
   584→
   585→        Any class attribute with an all-upper-case name is taken to be as an
   586→        enum value.
   587→    """
   588→
   589→    c_name: str
   590→    dtype: "numpy.dtype[Any]"
   591→    c_value_prefix: str
   592→
   593→    @classmethod
   594→    def get_flag_names_and_values(cls):
   595→        return [(name, getattr(cls, name))
   596→                for name in sorted(dir(cls))
   597→                if name[0].isupper()]
   598→
   599→    @classmethod
   600→    def get_c_defines_lines(cls):
   601→        return [
   602→                f"#define {cls.c_value_prefix}{flag_name} {value}"
   603→                for flag_name, value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()]
   604→
   605→    @classmethod
   606→    def get_c_defines(cls):
   607→        """Return a string with C defines corresponding to these constants.
   608→        """
   609→
   610→        return "\n".join(cls.get_c_defines_lines())
   611→
   612→    @classmethod
   613→    def get_c_typedef_line(cls):
   614→        """Returns a typedef to define this enum in C."""
   615→
   616→        from pyopencl.tools import dtype_to_ctype  # pylint: disable=import-error
   617→        return f"typedef {dtype_to_ctype(cls.dtype)} {cls.c_name};"
   618→
   619→    @classmethod
   620→    def get_c_typedef(cls):
   621→        return f"\n\n{cls.get_c_typedef_line()}\n\n"
   622→
   623→    @classmethod
   624→    def generate(cls):
   625→        yield cls.get_c_typedef_line()
   626→        yield from cls.get_c_defines_lines()
   627→
   628→    @classmethod
   629→    def stringify_value(cls, val):
   630→        """Return a string description of the flags set in *val*."""
   631→
   632→        return "|".join(
   633→                flag_name
   634→                for flag_name, flag_value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()
   635→                if val & flag_value)
   636→
   637→# }}}

   656→# {{{ control flow/statement stuff
   657→
   658→class If(Generable):
   659→    def __init__(self, condition, then_, else_=None):
   660→        self.condition = condition
   661→
   662→        assert isinstance(then_, Generable)
   663→        if else_ is not None:
   664→            assert isinstance(else_, Generable)
   665→
   666→        self.then_ = then_
   667→        self.else_ = else_
   668→
   669→    def generate(self):
   670→        cond_str = str(self.condition)
   671→        condition_lines = cond_str.split("\n")
   672→        if len(condition_lines) > 1:
   673→            yield "if ("
   674→            for line in condition_lines:
   675→                yield f"    {line}"
   676→            yield "  )"
   677→        else:
   678→            yield f"if ({self.condition})"
   679→
   680→        if isinstance(self.then_, Block):
   681→            for line in self.then_.generate():
   682→                yield line
   683→        else:
   684→            for line in self.then_.generate():
   685→                yield f"  {line}"
   686→
   687→        if self.else_ is not None:
   688→            yield "else"
   689→            if isinstance(self.else_, Block):
   690→                for line in self.else_.generate():
   691→                    yield line
   692→            else:
   693→                for line in self.else_.generate():
   694→                    yield f"  {line}"
   695→
   696→    mapper_method = "map_if"
   697→
   698→
   699→class Loop(Generable):
   700→    def __init__(self, body):
   701→        self.body = body
   702→
   703→    def intro_line(self):
   704→        raise NotImplementedError

  1006→class Block(Generable):

  1019→        for item in self.contents:

  1021→                yield f"  {item_line}"

  1048→class LiteralLines(Generable):
  1049→    def __init__(self, text):
  1050→        # accommodate pyopencl syntax highlighting
  1051→        if text.startswith("//CL//"):
  1052→            text = text[6:]
  1053→
  1054→        if not text.startswith("\n"):
  1055→            raise ValueError("expected newline as first character "
  1056→                    "in literal lines")
  1057→
  1058→        lines = text.split("\n")
  1059→        while lines[0].strip() == "":
  1060→            lines.pop(0)
  1061→        while lines[-1].strip() == "":
  1062→            lines.pop(-1)
  1063→
  1064→        if lines:
  1065→            base_indent = 0
  1066→            while lines[0][base_indent] in " \t":
  1067→                base_indent += 1
  1068→
  1069→            for line in lines[1:]:
  1070→                if line[:base_indent].strip():
  1071→                    raise ValueError("inconsistent indentation")
  1072→
  1073→        self.lines = [line[base_indent:] for line in lines]
  1074→
  1075→    def generate(self):
  1076→        yield from self.lines
  1077→
  1078→    mapper_method = "map_literal_lines"
  1079→

  1084→        for line in self.lines:
  1085→            yield f"  {line}"

  1134→class PrivateNamespace(Block):
  1135→    def get_namespace_name(self):
  1136→        try:
  1137→            import hashlib
  1138→            checksum = hashlib.md5()
  1139→        except ImportError:

  1141→            import md5
  1142→            checksum = md5.new()
  1143→
  1144→        for c in self.contents:
  1145→            for line in c.generate():
  1146→                checksum.update(line.encode("utf-8"))
  1147→

  1149→
  1150→    def generate(self):
  1151→        yield "namespace "+self.get_namespace_name()
  1152→        yield "{"
  1153→        for item in self.contents:
  1154→            for item_line in item.generate():
  1155→                yield f"  {item_line}"
  1156→        yield "}"
  1157→        yield ""
  1158→        yield f"using namespace {self.get_namespace_name()};"

    40→def is_long_64_bit():

    44→def dtype_to_ctype(dtype):
    45→    if dtype is None:
    46→        raise ValueError("dtype may not be None")
    47→
    48→    dtype = numpy.dtype(dtype)
    49→    if dtype == numpy.int64:
    50→        if is_long_64_bit():
    51→            return "long"
    52→        else:
    53→            return "long long"
    54→    elif dtype == numpy.uint64:
    55→        if is_long_64_bit():
    56→            return "unsigned long"
    57→        else:
    58→            return "unsigned long long"
    59→    elif dtype == numpy.int32:
    60→        return "int"
    61→    elif dtype == numpy.uint32:
    62→        return "unsigned int"
    63→    elif dtype == numpy.int16:
    64→        return "short int"
    65→    elif dtype == numpy.uint16:
    66→        return "short unsigned int"
    67→    elif dtype == numpy.int8:
    68→        return "signed char"
    69→    elif dtype == numpy.uint8:
    70→        return "unsigned char"
    71→    elif dtype == numpy.float32:
    72→        return "float"
    73→    elif dtype == numpy.float64:
    74→        return "double"
    75→    elif dtype == numpy.complex64:
    76→        return "std::complex<float>"
    77→    elif dtype == numpy.complex128:
    78→        return "std::complex<double>"
    79→    elif dtype == numpy.void:
    80→        return "void"
    81→    else:

    85→class Generable:

    99→class Declarator(Generable):
   100→    def generate(self, with_semicolon=True):
   101→        tp_lines, tp_decl = self.get_decl_pair()
   102→        tp_lines = list(tp_lines)
   103→        yield from tp_lines[:-1]
   104→        sc = ";"
   105→        if not with_semicolon:
   106→            sc = ""
   107→        if tp_decl is None:
   108→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]}{sc}"
   109→        else:
   110→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]} {tp_decl}{sc}"

   112→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   113→        """Return a tuple ``(type_lines, rhs)``.
   114→
   115→        *type_lines* is a non-empty list of lines (most often just a
   116→        single one) describing the type of this declarator. *rhs* is the right-
   117→        hand side that actually contains the function/array/constness notation
   118→        making up the bulk of the declarator syntax.
   119→        """
   120→        raise NotImplementedError

   122→    def inline(self, with_semicolon=True):

   125→        tp_lines = " ".join(tp_lines)
   126→        if tp_decl is None:
   127→            return tp_lines
   128→        else:
   129→            return f"{tp_lines} {tp_decl}"

   422→class Struct(Declarator):

   461→class GenerableStruct(Struct):
   462→    def __init__(self, tpname, fields, declname=None,

   465→        *tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
   466→        name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
   467→        padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
   468→        *fields* is a list of :class:`Declarator` instances.
   469→
   470→        *align_bytes* is an integer that causes the structure to be
   471→        padded to an integer multiple of itself.
   472→        *aligned_prime_to* is a list of integers.
   473→        If the resulting structure's size
   474→        is ``s``, then ``s//align_bytes`` will be made prime to all
   475→        numbers in *aligned_prime_to*. (Sounds obscure? It's needed
   476→        for avoiding bank conflicts in CUDA programming.)
   477→        """
   478→        if aligned_prime_to is None:
   479→            aligned_prime_to = []
   480→
   481→        format = "".join(f.struct_format() for f in fields)
   482→        bytes = _struct.calcsize(format)
   483→
   484→        natural_align_bytes = max(f.alignment_requirement() for f in fields)
   485→        if align_bytes is None:
   486→            align_bytes = natural_align_bytes
   487→        elif align_bytes < natural_align_bytes:
   488→            from warnings import warn
   489→            warn("requested struct alignment smaller than natural alignment",
   490→                 stacklevel=2)
   491→
   492→        self.align_bytes = align_bytes
   493→
   494→        padded_bytes = ((bytes + align_bytes - 1) // align_bytes) * align_bytes
   495→
   496→        def satisfies_primality(n):
   497→            import math
   498→            for p in aligned_prime_to:
   499→                if math.gcd(n, p) != 1:
   500→                    return False
   501→            return True
   502→
   503→        while not satisfies_primality(padded_bytes // align_bytes):
   504→            padded_bytes += align_bytes

   507→
   508→        if self.pad_bytes:
   509→            self.format = format + f"{self.pad_bytes}x"
   510→            self.bytes = padded_bytes
   511→        else:

   513→            self.bytes = bytes
   514→

   525→        """Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
   526→        :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
   527→        in *kwargs*.
   528→        """

   533→        :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
   534→        in *kwargs*.
   535→
   536→        Unlike :meth:`make`, not all members have to occur in *kwargs*.
   537→        """
   538→        return self._maker(with_defaults=True)(_struct.pack, **kwargs)

   540→    @memoize_method
   541→    def _maker(self, with_defaults=False):
   542→        def format_arg(f):
   543→            if with_defaults:
   544→                return f"{f.name}={f.default_value()!r}"
   545→            else:
   546→                return f.name
   547→
   548→        code = "lambda pack, {}: pack({}, {})".format(
   549→                ", ".join(format_arg(f) for f in self.fields),
   550→                repr(self.struct_format()),
   551→                ", ".join(f.struct_maker_code(f.name) for f in self.fields))

   556→        module.

   577→    .. attribute:: c_name
   578→    .. attribute:: dtype
   579→    .. attribute:: c_value_prefix
   580→
   581→        A (usually upper-case) prefix to be used as a prefix to the names defined
   582→        on the Python side.
   583→    .. attribute:: VALUE
   584→
   585→        Any class attribute with an all-upper-case name is taken to be as an
   586→        enum value.
   587→    """
   588→
   589→    c_name: str
   590→    dtype: "numpy.dtype[Any]"
   591→    c_value_prefix: str
   592→
   593→    @classmethod
   594→    def get_flag_names_and_values(cls):
   595→        return [(name, getattr(cls, name))
   596→                for name in sorted(dir(cls))
   597→                if name[0].isupper()]
   598→
   599→    @classmethod
   600→    def get_c_defines_lines(cls):
   601→        return [
   602→                f"#define {cls.c_value_prefix}{flag_name} {value}"
   603→                for flag_name, value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()]
   604→
   605→    @classmethod
   606→    def get_c_defines(cls):
   607→        """Return a string with C defines corresponding to these constants.
   608→        """
   609→
   610→        return "\n".join(cls.get_c_defines_lines())
   611→
   612→    @classmethod
   613→    def get_c_typedef_line(cls):
   614→        """Returns a typedef to define this enum in C."""
   615→
   616→        from pyopencl.tools import dtype_to_ctype  # pylint: disable=import-error
   617→        return f"typedef {dtype_to_ctype(cls.dtype)} {cls.c_name};"
   618→
   619→    @classmethod
   620→    def get_c_typedef(cls):
   621→        return f"\n\n{cls.get_c_typedef_line()}\n\n"
   622→
   623→    @classmethod
   624→    def generate(cls):
   625→        yield cls.get_c_typedef_line()
   626→        yield from cls.get_c_defines_lines()
   627→
   628→    @classmethod
   629→    def stringify_value(cls, val):
   630→        """Return a string description of the flags set in *val*."""
   631→
   632→        return "|".join(
   633→                flag_name
   634→                for flag_name, flag_value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()
   635→                if val & flag_value)
   636→
   637→# }}}
   638→
   639→
   640→# {{{ template
   641→
   642→class Template(NestedDeclarator):
   643→    def __init__(self, template_spec, subdecl):
   644→        self.template_spec = template_spec
   645→        self.subdecl = subdecl
   646→
   647→    def generate(self, with_semicolon=False):
   648→        yield f"template <{self.template_spec}>"
   649→        yield from self.subdecl.generate(with_semicolon)
   650→
   651→    mapper_method = "map_template"
   652→
   653→# }}}

   656→# {{{ control flow/statement stuff
   657→
   658→class If(Generable):
   659→    def __init__(self, condition, then_, else_=None):
   660→        self.condition = condition
   661→
   662→        assert isinstance(then_, Generable)
   663→        if else_ is not None:
   664→            assert isinstance(else_, Generable)
   665→
   666→        self.then_ = then_
   667→        self.else_ = else_
   668→
   669→    def generate(self):
   670→        cond_str = str(self.condition)
   671→        condition_lines = cond_str.split("\n")
   672→        if len(condition_lines) > 1:
   673→            yield "if ("
   674→            for line in condition_lines:
   675→                yield f"    {line}"
   676→            yield "  )"
   677→        else:
   678→            yield f"if ({self.condition})"
   679→
   680→        if isinstance(self.then_, Block):
   681→            for line in self.then_.generate():
   682→                yield line
   683→        else:
   684→            for line in self.then_.generate():
   685→                yield f"  {line}"
   686→
   687→        if self.else_ is not None:
   688→            yield "else"
   689→            if isinstance(self.else_, Block):
   690→                for line in self.else_.generate():
   691→                    yield line
   692→            else:
   693→                for line in self.else_.generate():
   694→                    yield f"  {line}"
   695→
   696→    mapper_method = "map_if"
   697→
   698→
   699→class Loop(Generable):
   700→    def __init__(self, body):
   701→        self.body = body
   702→
   703→    def intro_line(self):
   704→        raise NotImplementedError
   705→
   706→    def generate(self):
   707→        if self.intro_line() is not None:
   708→            yield self.intro_line()
   709→
   710→        if isinstance(self.body, Block):
   711→            for line in self.body.generate():
   712→                yield line
   713→        else:
   714→            for line in self.body.generate():
   715→                yield "  "+line
   716→
   717→        if self.outro_line() is not None:
   718→            yield self.outro_line()
   719→
   720→    def outro_line(self):
   721→        return None
   722→
   723→
   724→class CustomLoop(Loop):
   725→    def __init__(self, intro_line, body, outro_line=None):
   726→        self.intro_line_ = intro_line
   727→        self.body = body
   728→        self.outro_line_ = outro_line
   729→
   730→    def intro_line(self):
   731→        return self.intro_line_
   732→
   733→    def outro_line(self):
   734→        return self.outro_line_
   735→
   736→    mapper_method = "map_custom_loop"
   737→
   738→
   739→class While(Loop):
   740→    def __init__(self, condition, body):
   741→        self.condition = condition
   742→        assert isinstance(body, Generable)
   743→        self.body = body
   744→
   745→    def intro_line(self):
   746→        return f"while ({self.condition})"
   747→
   748→    mapper_method = "map_while"
   749→
   750→
   751→class For(Loop):
   752→    def __init__(self, start, condition, update, body):
   753→        self.start = start
   754→        self.condition = condition
   755→        self.update = update
   756→
   757→        assert isinstance(body, Generable)
   758→        self.body = body
   759→
   760→    def intro_line(self):
   761→        return f"for ({self.start}; {self.condition}; {self.update})"
   762→
   763→    mapper_method = "map_for"
   764→
   765→
   766→class DoWhile(Loop):
   767→    def __init__(self, condition, body):
   768→        self.condition = condition
   769→        assert isinstance(body, Generable)
   770→        self.body = body
   771→
   772→    def intro_line(self):
   773→        return "do"
   774→
   775→    def outro_line(self):
   776→        return f"while ({self.condition});"
   777→
   778→    mapper_method = "map_do_while"
   779→
   780→
   781→def make_multiple_ifs(conditions_and_blocks, base=None):
   782→    if base == "last":
   783→        _, base = conditions_and_blocks[-1]
   784→        conditions_and_blocks = conditions_and_blocks[:-1]
   785→
   786→    for cond, block in conditions_and_blocks[::-1]:
   787→        base = If(cond, block, base)
   788→    return base
   789→

   861→class Line(Generable):

   871→class Comment(Generable):
   872→    def __init__(self, text, skip_space=False):
   873→        self.text = text
   874→        if skip_space:
   875→            self.fmt_str = "/*{text}*/"  # noqa: RUF027
   876→        else:
   877→            self.fmt_str = "/* {text} */"  # noqa: RUF027
   878→
   879→    def generate(self):
   880→        yield self.fmt_str.format(text=self.text)
   881→
   882→    mapper_method = "map_comment"
   883→
   884→
   885→class MultilineComment(Generable):
   886→    def __init__(self, text, skip_space=False):
   887→        self.text = text
   888→        self.skip_space = skip_space
   889→
   890→    def generate(self):
   891→        yield "/**"
   892→        if self.skip_space is True:
   893→            line_begin, comment_end = "*", "*/"
   894→        else:
   895→            line_begin, comment_end = " * ", " */"
   896→        for line in self.text.splitlines():
   897→            yield line_begin + line
   898→        yield comment_end
   899→
   900→    mapper_method = "map_multiline_comment"
   901→
   902→
   903→class LineComment(Generable):
   904→    def __init__(self, text):
   905→        assert "\n" not in text
   906→        self.text = text
   907→
   908→    def generate(self):
   909→        yield f"// {self.text}"
   910→
   911→    mapper_method = "map_line_comment"
   912→
   913→
   914→def add_comment(comment, stmt):
   915→    if comment is None:
   916→        return stmt
   917→
   918→    if isinstance(stmt, Block):
   919→        result = Block([Comment(comment), Line()])
   920→        result.extend(stmt.contents)
   921→        return result
   922→    else:
   923→        return Block([Comment(comment), Line(), stmt])

   928→# {{{ initializers
   929→
   930→class Initializer(Generable):
   931→    def __init__(self, vdecl, data):
   932→        self.vdecl = vdecl
   933→        self.data = data
   934→
   935→    def generate(self):
   936→        tp_lines, tp_decl = self.vdecl.get_decl_pair()
   937→        tp_lines = list(tp_lines)
   938→        yield from tp_lines[:-1]
   939→        if isinstance(self.data, str) and "\n" in self.data:
   940→            data_lines = self.data.split("\n")
   941→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]} {tp_decl} ="
   942→            for i, line in enumerate(data_lines):
   943→                if i == len(data_lines)-1:
   944→                    yield f"  {line};"
   945→                else:
   946→                    yield f"  {line}"
   947→        else:
   948→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]} {tp_decl} = {self.data};"
   949→
   950→    mapper_method = "map_initializer"
   951→
   952→
   953→class InlineInitializer(Initializer):
   954→    """
   955→    Class to represent Initializers (int i=0) without the semicolon in the end
   956→    (e.g. in a for statement)
   957→    Usage: same as cgen.Initializer
   958→    Result: same as cgen.Initializer except for the lack of a semi-colon at the end
   959→    """
   960→    def generate(self):
   961→        result = super().generate()
   962→        for v in result:
   963→            if v.endswith(";"):
   964→                yield v[:-1]
   965→            else:
   966→                yield v
   967→
   968→
   969→def Constant(vdecl, data):  # noqa
   970→    return Initializer(Const(vdecl), data)
   971→
   972→
   973→class ArrayInitializer(Generable):
   974→    def __init__(self, vdecl, data):
   975→        self.vdecl = vdecl
   976→        self.data = data
   977→
   978→    def generate(self):
   979→        yield from self.vdecl.generate(with_semicolon=False)
   980→        yield "  = { %s };" % (", ".join(str(item) for item in self.data))
   981→
   982→    mapper_method = "map_array_initializer"

   984→
   985→class FunctionBody(Generable):
   986→    def __init__(self, fdecl, body):
   987→        """Initialize a function definition. *fdecl* is expected to be
   988→        a :class:`FunctionDeclaration` instance, while *body* is a
   989→        :class:`Block`.
   990→        """
   991→
   992→        self.fdecl = fdecl
   993→        self.body = body
   994→
   995→    def generate(self):
   996→        yield from self.fdecl.generate(with_semicolon=False)
   997→        yield from self.body.generate()
   998→
   999→    mapper_method = "map_function_body"

  1004→# {{{ block
  1005→
  1006→class Block(Generable):
  1007→    def __init__(self, contents=None):
  1008→        if contents is None:
  1009→            contents = []
  1010→        if isinstance(contents, Block):
  1011→            contents = contents.contents
  1012→        self.contents = contents[:]
  1013→
  1014→        for item in contents:
  1015→            assert isinstance(item, Generable)
  1016→
  1017→    def generate(self):
  1018→        yield "{"
  1019→        for item in self.contents:
  1020→            for item_line in item.generate():
  1021→                yield f"  {item_line}"
  1022→        yield "}"
  1023→
  1024→    def append(self, data):
  1025→        self.contents.append(data)
  1026→
  1027→    def extend(self, data):
  1028→        self.contents.extend(data)
  1029→
  1030→    def insert(self, i, data):
  1031→        self.contents.insert(i, data)
  1032→
  1033→    def extend_log_block(self, descr, data):
  1034→        self.contents.append(Comment(descr))
  1035→        self.contents.extend(data)
  1036→        self.contents.append(Line())
  1037→
  1038→    mapper_method = "map_block"
  1039→
  1040→
  1041→def block_if_necessary(contents):
  1042→    if len(contents) == 1:
  1043→        return contents[0]
  1044→    else:
  1045→        return Block(contents)
  1046→

  1048→class LiteralLines(Generable):
  1049→    def __init__(self, text):
  1050→        # accommodate pyopencl syntax highlighting
  1051→        if text.startswith("//CL//"):
  1052→            text = text[6:]
  1053→
  1054→        if not text.startswith("\n"):
  1055→            raise ValueError("expected newline as first character "
  1056→                    "in literal lines")
  1057→
  1058→        lines = text.split("\n")
  1059→        while lines[0].strip() == "":
  1060→            lines.pop(0)
  1061→        while lines[-1].strip() == "":
  1062→            lines.pop(-1)
  1063→
  1064→        if lines:
  1065→            base_indent = 0
  1066→            while lines[0][base_indent] in " \t":
  1067→                base_indent += 1
  1068→
  1069→            for line in lines[1:]:
  1070→                if line[:base_indent].strip():
  1071→                    raise ValueError("inconsistent indentation")
  1072→
  1073→        self.lines = [line[base_indent:] for line in lines]
  1074→
  1075→    def generate(self):
  1076→        yield from self.lines
  1077→
  1078→    mapper_method = "map_literal_lines"
  1079→
  1080→
  1081→class LiteralBlock(LiteralLines):
  1082→    def generate(self):
  1083→        yield "{"
  1084→        for line in self.lines:
  1085→            yield f"  {line}"
  1086→        yield "}"
  1087→
  1088→
  1089→class Collection(Block):
  1090→    def generate(self):
  1091→        for c in self.contents:
  1092→            yield from c.generate()
  1093→
  1094→
  1095→Module = Collection
  1096→
  1097→
  1098→class IfDef(Module):
  1099→    """
  1100→    Class to represent IfDef-Else-EndIf construct for the C preprocessor.
  1101→    :param condition: the condition in IfDef
  1102→    :param iflines: the block of code inside the if [an array of type Generable]
  1103→    :param elselines: the block of code inside the else [an array of type Generable]
  1104→    """
  1105→    def __init__(self, condition, iflines, elselines):
  1106→        ifdef_line = Line(f"#ifdef {condition}")
  1107→        if len(elselines):
  1108→            elselines.insert(0, Line("#else"))
  1109→        endif_line = Line("#endif")
  1110→        lines = [ifdef_line, *iflines, *elselines, endif_line]
  1111→        super().__init__(lines)
  1112→
  1113→    mapper_method = "map_ifdef"
  1114→
  1115→
  1116→class IfNDef(Module):
  1117→    """
  1118→    Class to represent IfNDef-Else-EndIf construct for the C preprocessor.
  1119→    :param condition: the condition in IfNDef
  1120→    :param ifndeflines: the block of code inside the if not
  1121→        [an array of type Generable]
  1122→    :param elselines: the block of code inside the else [an array of type Generable]
  1123→    """
  1124→    def __init__(self, condition, ifndeflines, elselines):
  1125→        ifndefdef_line = Line(f"#ifndef {condition}")
  1126→        if len(elselines):
  1127→            elselines.insert(0, Line("#else"))
  1128→        lines = [ifndefdef_line, *ifndeflines, *elselines, Line("#endif")]
  1129→        super().__init__(lines)
  1130→
  1131→    mapper_method = "map_ifndef"
  1132→
  1133→
  1134→class PrivateNamespace(Block):
  1135→    def get_namespace_name(self):
  1136→        try:
  1137→            import hashlib
  1138→            checksum = hashlib.md5()
  1139→        except ImportError:
  1140→            # for Python << 2.5
  1141→            import md5
  1142→            checksum = md5.new()
  1143→
  1144→        for c in self.contents:
  1145→            for line in c.generate():
  1146→                checksum.update(line.encode("utf-8"))
  1147→
  1148→        return "private_namespace_"+checksum.hexdigest()
  1149→
  1150→    def generate(self):
  1151→        yield "namespace "+self.get_namespace_name()
  1152→        yield "{"
  1153→        for item in self.contents:
  1154→            for item_line in item.generate():
  1155→                yield f"  {item_line}"
  1156→        yield "}"
  1157→        yield ""
  1158→        yield f"using namespace {self.get_namespace_name()};"
  1159→
  1160→# }}}

    38→
    39→@memoize
    40→def is_long_64_bit():
    41→    return _struct.calcsize("l") == 8
    42→
    43→
    44→def dtype_to_ctype(dtype):
    45→    if dtype is None:
    46→        raise ValueError("dtype may not be None")
    47→
    48→    dtype = numpy.dtype(dtype)
    49→    if dtype == numpy.int64:
    50→        if is_long_64_bit():
    51→            return "long"
    52→        else:
    53→            return "long long"
    54→    elif dtype == numpy.uint64:
    55→        if is_long_64_bit():
    56→            return "unsigned long"
    57→        else:
    58→            return "unsigned long long"
    59→    elif dtype == numpy.int32:
    60→        return "int"
    61→    elif dtype == numpy.uint32:
    62→        return "unsigned int"
    63→    elif dtype == numpy.int16:
    64→        return "short int"
    65→    elif dtype == numpy.uint16:
    66→        return "short unsigned int"
    67→    elif dtype == numpy.int8:
    68→        return "signed char"
    69→    elif dtype == numpy.uint8:
    70→        return "unsigned char"
    71→    elif dtype == numpy.float32:
    72→        return "float"
    73→    elif dtype == numpy.float64:
    74→        return "double"
    75→    elif dtype == numpy.complex64:
    76→        return "std::complex<float>"
    77→    elif dtype == numpy.complex128:
    78→        return "std::complex<double>"
    79→    elif dtype == numpy.void:
    80→        return "void"
    81→    else:
    82→        raise ValueError(f"unable to map dtype '{dtype}'")
    83→
    84→
    85→class Generable:
    86→    def __str__(self):
    87→        """Return a single string (possibly containing newlines) representing
    88→        this code construct."""
    89→        return "\n".join(line.rstrip() for line in self.generate())
    90→
    91→    def generate(self, with_semicolon=True):
    92→        """Generate (i.e. yield) the lines making up this code construct."""
    93→
    94→        raise NotImplementedError
    95→
    96→
    97→# {{{ declarators
    98→
    99→class Declarator(Generable):
   100→    def generate(self, with_semicolon=True):
   101→        tp_lines, tp_decl = self.get_decl_pair()
   102→        tp_lines = list(tp_lines)
   103→        yield from tp_lines[:-1]
   104→        sc = ";"
   105→        if not with_semicolon:
   106→            sc = ""
   107→        if tp_decl is None:
   108→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]}{sc}"
   109→        else:
   110→            yield f"{tp_lines[-1]} {tp_decl}{sc}"
   111→
   112→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   113→        """Return a tuple ``(type_lines, rhs)``.
   114→
   115→        *type_lines* is a non-empty list of lines (most often just a
   116→        single one) describing the type of this declarator. *rhs* is the right-
   117→        hand side that actually contains the function/array/constness notation
   118→        making up the bulk of the declarator syntax.
   119→        """
   120→        raise NotImplementedError
   121→
   122→    def inline(self, with_semicolon=True):
   123→        """Return the declarator as a single line."""
   124→        tp_lines, tp_decl = self.get_decl_pair()
   125→        tp_lines = " ".join(tp_lines)
   126→        if tp_decl is None:
   127→            return tp_lines
   128→        else:
   129→            return f"{tp_lines} {tp_decl}"
   130→
   131→
   132→class POD(Declarator):
   133→    """A simple declarator: The type is given as a :class:`numpy.dtype`
   134→    and the *name* is given as a string.
   135→    """
   136→
   137→    def __init__(self, dtype, name):
   138→        self.dtype = numpy.dtype(dtype)
   139→        self.name = name
   140→
   141→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   142→        return [dtype_to_ctype(self.dtype)], self.name
   143→
   144→    def struct_maker_code(self, name):
   145→        return name
   146→
   147→    def struct_format(self):
   148→        return self.dtype.char
   149→
   150→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   151→        return _struct.calcsize(self.struct_format())
   152→
   153→    def default_value(self):
   154→        return 0
   155→
   156→    mapper_method = "map_pod"

   159→class Value(Declarator):
   160→    """A simple declarator: *typename* and *name* are given as strings."""
   161→
   162→    def __init__(self, typename, name):
   163→        self.typename = typename
   164→        self.name = name

   166→    def get_decl_pair(self):

   181→class NestedDeclarator(Declarator):
   182→    def __init__(self, subdecl):
   183→        self.subdecl = subdecl
   184→
   185→    @property
   186→    def name(self):
   187→        return self.subdecl.name
   188→
   189→    def struct_format(self):
   190→        return self.subdecl.struct_format()
   191→
   192→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   193→        return self.subdecl.alignment_requirement()
   194→
   195→    def struct_maker_code(self, data):
   196→        return self.subdecl.struct_maker_code(data)
   197→
   198→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   199→        return self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   200→
   201→
   202→class DeclSpecifier(NestedDeclarator):
   203→    def __init__(self, subdecl, spec, sep=" "):
   204→        NestedDeclarator.__init__(self, subdecl)
   205→        self.spec = spec
   206→        self.sep = sep
   207→
   208→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   209→        def add_spec(sub_it):
   210→            it = iter(sub_it)
   211→            try:
   212→                yield f"{self.spec}{self.sep}{next(it)}"
   213→            except StopIteration:
   214→                pass
   215→
   216→            yield from it
   217→
   218→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   219→        return add_spec(sub_tp), sub_decl
   220→
   221→
   222→class NamespaceQualifier(DeclSpecifier):
   223→    def __init__(self, namespace, subdecl):
   224→        DeclSpecifier.__init__(self, subdecl, namespace, "::")
   225→
   226→    mapper_method = "map_namespace_qualifier"

   288→class AlignedAttribute(NestedDeclarator):
   289→    """
   290→    Assigns an alignment for a definition of a type or an array.
   291→    """
   292→    def __init__(self, align_bytes, subdecl):
   293→        super().__init__(subdecl)
   294→        self.align_bytes = align_bytes
   295→
   296→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   297→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()

   303→class AlignValueAttribute(NestedDeclarator):
   304→    """
   305→    Assigns an alignment for value of a pointer.
   306→
   307→    This is used for pointers where the user guarantees to the compiler that the
   308→    value of the pointer has the alignment stated. :class:`AlignedAttribute` on
   309→    the other hand tells the compiler to declare the type or the array with the
   310→    given alignment and cannot be used for telling the compiler that an existing
   311→    pointer has a certain alignment guarantee.
   312→    This attribute is currently supported by clang [1] and Intel [2] and is ignored
   313→    by gcc.
   314→
   315→    [1]: https://reviews.llvm.org/D4635
   316→    [2]: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/develop/documentation/oneapi-dpcpp-cpp-compiler-dev-guide-and-reference/top/compiler-reference/attributes/align-value.html
   317→    """
   318→    def __init__(self, align_bytes, subdecl):
   319→        super().__init__(subdecl)
   320→        self.align_bytes = align_bytes
   321→
   322→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   323→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   324→        return (sub_tp,
   325→            f"{sub_decl} __attribute__ ((align_value ({self.align_bytes})))")
   326→
   327→    mapper_method = "map_align_value"
   328→

   366→class ArrayOf(NestedDeclarator):
   367→    def __init__(self, subdecl, count=None):
   368→        NestedDeclarator.__init__(self, subdecl)
   369→        self.count = count
   370→
   371→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   372→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   373→        if self.count is None:
   374→            count_str = ""
   375→        else:
   376→            count_str = str(self.count)
   377→        return sub_tp, (f"{sub_decl}[{count_str}]")
   378→
   379→    def struct_maker_code(self, name):
   380→        return ", ".join(f"{name}[{i}]" for i in range(self.count))
   381→
   382→    def struct_format(self):
   383→        if self.count is None:
   384→            return "P"
   385→        else:
   386→            return f"{self.count}{self.subdecl.struct_format()}"
   387→
   388→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   389→        return self.subdecl.alignment_requirement()
   390→
   391→    def default_value(self):
   392→        return self.count*[self.subdecl.default_value()]
   393→
   394→    mapper_method = "map_array_of"

   397→class FunctionDeclaration(NestedDeclarator):
   398→    def __init__(self, subdecl, arg_decls):
   399→        NestedDeclarator.__init__(self, subdecl)
   400→        self.arg_decls = arg_decls
   401→
   402→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   403→        sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
   404→
   405→        return sub_tp, ("{}({})".format(
   406→            sub_decl,
   407→            ", ".join(ad.inline() for ad in self.arg_decls)))
   408→
   409→    def struct_maker_code(self, data):
   410→        raise RuntimeError("function pointers can't be put into structs")
   411→
   412→    def struct_format(self):
   413→        raise RuntimeError("function pointers have no struct format")
   414→
   415→    mapper_method = "map_function_declaration"

   419→
   420→# {{{ struct-like
   421→
   422→class Struct(Declarator):
   423→    """A structure declarator."""
   424→
   425→    def __init__(self, tpname, fields, declname=None, pad_bytes=0):
   426→        """Initialize the structure declarator.
   427→        *tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
   428→        name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
   429→        padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
   430→        *fields* is a list of :class:`Declarator` instances.
   431→        """
   432→        self.tpname = tpname
   433→        self.fields = fields
   434→        self.declname = declname
   435→        self.pad_bytes = pad_bytes
   436→
   437→    def get_decl_pair(self):
   438→        def get_tp():
   439→            if self.tpname is not None:
   440→                yield f"struct {self.tpname}"
   441→            else:
   442→                yield "struct"
   443→            yield "{"
   444→            for f in self.fields:
   445→                for f_line in f.generate():
   446→                    yield f"  {f_line}"
   447→            if self.pad_bytes:
   448→                yield f"  unsigned char _cgen_pad[{self.pad_bytes}];"
   449→            yield "} " + self.struct_attributes()
   450→        return get_tp(), self.declname
   451→
   452→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   453→        return max(f.alignment_requirement() for f in self.fields)
   454→
   455→    def struct_attributes(self):
   456→        return ""
   457→
   458→    mapper_method = "map_struct"
   459→
   460→
   461→class GenerableStruct(Struct):
   462→    def __init__(self, tpname, fields, declname=None,
   463→            align_bytes=None, aligned_prime_to=None):
   464→        """Initialize a structure declarator.
   465→        *tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
   466→        name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
   467→        padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
   468→        *fields* is a list of :class:`Declarator` instances.
   469→
   470→        *align_bytes* is an integer that causes the structure to be
   471→        padded to an integer multiple of itself.
   472→        *aligned_prime_to* is a list of integers.
   473→        If the resulting structure's size
   474→        is ``s``, then ``s//align_bytes`` will be made prime to all
   475→        numbers in *aligned_prime_to*. (Sounds obscure? It's needed
   476→        for avoiding bank conflicts in CUDA programming.)
   477→        """
   478→        if aligned_prime_to is None:
   479→            aligned_prime_to = []
   480→
   481→        format = "".join(f.struct_format() for f in fields)
   482→        bytes = _struct.calcsize(format)
   483→
   484→        natural_align_bytes = max(f.alignment_requirement() for f in fields)
   485→        if align_bytes is None:
   486→            align_bytes = natural_align_bytes
   487→        elif align_bytes < natural_align_bytes:
   488→            from warnings import warn
   489→            warn("requested struct alignment smaller than natural alignment",
   490→                 stacklevel=2)
   491→
   492→        self.align_bytes = align_bytes
   493→
   494→        padded_bytes = ((bytes + align_bytes - 1) // align_bytes) * align_bytes
   495→
   496→        def satisfies_primality(n):
   497→            import math
   498→            for p in aligned_prime_to:
   499→                if math.gcd(n, p) != 1:
   500→                    return False
   501→            return True
   502→
   503→        while not satisfies_primality(padded_bytes // align_bytes):
   504→            padded_bytes += align_bytes
   505→
   506→        Struct.__init__(self, tpname, fields, declname, padded_bytes - bytes)
   507→
   508→        if self.pad_bytes:
   509→            self.format = format + f"{self.pad_bytes}x"
   510→            self.bytes = padded_bytes
   511→        else:
   512→            self.format = format
   513→            self.bytes = bytes
   514→
   515→        assert _struct.calcsize(self.format) == self.bytes
   516→
   517→    # until nvcc bug is fixed
   518→    # def struct_attributes(self):
   519→    #     return "__attribute__ ((packed))"
   520→
   521→    def alignment_requirement(self):
   522→        return self.align_bytes
   523→
   524→    def make(self, **kwargs):
   525→        """Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
   526→        :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
   527→        in *kwargs*.
   528→        """
   529→        return self._maker()(_struct.pack, **kwargs)
   530→
   531→    def make_with_defaults(self, **kwargs):
   532→        """Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
   533→        :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
   534→        in *kwargs*.
   535→
   536→        Unlike :meth:`make`, not all members have to occur in *kwargs*.
   537→        """
   538→        return self._maker(with_defaults=True)(_struct.pack, **kwargs)
   539→
   540→    @memoize_method
   541→    def _maker(self, with_defaults=False):
   542→        def format_arg(f):
   543→            if with_defaults:
   544→                return f"{f.name}={f.default_value()!r}"
   545→            else:
   546→                return f.name
   547→
   548→        code = "lambda pack, {}: pack({}, {})".format(
   549→                ", ".join(format_arg(f) for f in self.fields),
   550→                repr(self.struct_format()),
   551→                ", ".join(f.struct_maker_code(f.name) for f in self.fields))
   552→        return eval(code)
   553→
   554→    def struct_format(self):
   555→        """Return the format of the struct as digested by the :mod:`struct`
   556→        module.
   557→        """
   558→        return self.format
   559→
   560→    def __len__(self):
   561→        """Return the number of bytes occupied by this struct."""
   562→        return self.bytes
   563→
   564→    mapper_method = "map_generable_struct"
   565→
   566→
   567→class Enum(Generable):
   568→    """An enum-like class for Python that can generate an equivalent C-level
   569→    declaration. Does not work within the usual "declarator" framework
   570→    because it uses macros to define values, and a separate typedef to define the
   571→    value type.
   572→
   573→    .. versionadded:: 2013.2
   574→
   575→    To use, derive from this class, and define the following things:
   576→
   577→    .. attribute:: c_name
   578→    .. attribute:: dtype
   579→    .. attribute:: c_value_prefix
   580→
   581→        A (usually upper-case) prefix to be used as a prefix to the names defined
   582→        on the Python side.
   583→    .. attribute:: VALUE
   584→
   585→        Any class attribute with an all-upper-case name is taken to be as an
   586→        enum value.
   587→    """
   588→
   589→    c_name: str
   590→    dtype: "numpy.dtype[Any]"
   591→    c_value_prefix: str
   592→
   593→    @classmethod
   594→    def get_flag_names_and_values(cls):
   595→        return [(name, getattr(cls, name))
   596→                for name in sorted(dir(cls))
   597→                if name[0].isupper()]
   598→
   599→    @classmethod
   600→    def get_c_defines_lines(cls):
   601→        return [
   602→                f"#define {cls.c_value_prefix}{flag_name} {value}"
   603→                for flag_name, value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()]
   604→
   605→    @classmethod
   606→    def get_c_defines(cls):
   607→        """Return a string with C defines corresponding to these constants.
   608→        """
   609→
   610→        return "\n".join(cls.get_c_defines_lines())
   611→
   612→    @classmethod
   613→    def get_c_typedef_line(cls):
   614→        """Returns a typedef to define this enum in C."""
   615→
   616→        from pyopencl.tools import dtype_to_ctype  # pylint: disable=import-error
   617→        return f"typedef {dtype_to_ctype(cls.dtype)} {cls.c_name};"
   618→
   619→    @classmethod
   620→    def get_c_typedef(cls):
   621→        return f"\n\n{cls.get_c_typedef_line()}\n\n"
   622→
   623→    @classmethod
   624→    def generate(cls):
   625→        yield cls.get_c_typedef_line()
   626→        yield from cls.get_c_defines_lines()
   627→
   628→    @classmethod
   629→    def stringify_value(cls, val):
   630→        """Return a string description of the flags set in *val*."""
   631→
   632→        return "|".join(
   633→                flag_name
   634→                for flag_name, flag_value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()
   635→                if val & flag_value)
   636→
   637→# }}}

   642→class Template(NestedDeclarator):
   643→    def __init__(self, template_spec, subdecl):
   644→        self.template_spec = template_spec
   645→        self.subdecl = subdecl
   646→
   647→    def generate(self, with_semicolon=False):
   648→        yield f"template <{self.template_spec}>"
   649→        yield from self.subdecl.generate(with_semicolon)
   650→
   651→    mapper_method = "map_template"

   656→# {{{ control flow/statement stuff
   657→
   658→class If(Generable):
   659→    def __init__(self, condition, then_, else_=None):
   660→        self.condition = condition
   661→
   662→        assert isinstance(then_, Generable)
   663→        if else_ is not None:
   664→            assert isinstance(else_, Generable)
   665→
   666→        self.then_ = then_
   667→        self.else_ = else_
   668→
   669→    def generate(self):
   670→        cond_str = str(self.condition)
   671→        condition_lines = cond_str.split("\n")
   672→        if len(condition_lines) > 1:
   673→            yield "if ("
   674→            for line in condition_lines:
   675→                yield f"    {line}"
   676→            yield "  )"
   677→        else:
   678→            yield f"if ({self.condition})"
   679→
   680→        if isinstance(self.then_, Block):
   681→            for line in self.then_.generate():
   682→                yield line
   683→        else:
   684→            for line in self.then_.generate():
   685→                yield f"  {line}"
   686→
   687→        if self.else_ is not None:
   688→            yield "else"
   689→            if isinstance(self.else_, Block):
   690→                for line in self.else_.generate():
   691→                    yield line
   692→            else:
   693→                for line in self.else_.generate():
   694→                    yield f"  {line}"
   695→
   696→    mapper_method = "map_if"
   697→
   698→
   699→class Loop(Generable):
   700→    def __init__(self, body):
   701→        self.body = body
   702→
   703→    def intro_line(self):
   704→        raise NotImplementedError
   705→
   706→    def generate(self):
   707→        if self.intro_line() is not None:
   708→            yield self.intro_line()
   709→
   710→        if isinstance(self.body, Block):
   711→            for line in self.body.generate():
   712→                yield line
   713→        else:
   714→            for line in self.body.generate():
   715→                yield "  "+line
   716→

   781→def make_multiple_ifs(conditions_and_blocks, base=None):
   782→    if base == "last":
   783→        _, base = conditions_and_blocks[-1]

   875→            self.fmt_str = "/*{text}*/"  # noqa: RUF027
   876→        else:
   877→            self.fmt_str = "/* {text} */"  # noqa: RUF027

   886→    def __init__(self, text, skip_space=False):
   887→        self.text = text
   888→        self.skip_space = skip_space

   891→        yield "/**"
   892→        if self.skip_space is True:
   893→            line_begin, comment_end = "*", "*/"
   894→        else:
   895→            line_begin, comment_end = " * ", " */"
   896→        for line in self.text.splitlines():
   897→            yield line_begin + line
   898→        yield comment_end

   903→class LineComment(Generable):
   904→    def __init__(self, text):
   905→        assert "\n" not in text
   906→        self.text = text

   909→        yield f"// {self.text}"

   911→    mapper_method = "map_line_comment"
   912→
   913→
   914→def add_comment(comment, stmt):
   915→    if comment is None:
   916→        return stmt
   917→
   918→    if isinstance(stmt, Block):
   919→        result = Block([Comment(comment), Line()])
   920→        result.extend(stmt.contents)
   921→        return result
   922→    else:
   923→        return Block([Comment(comment), Line(), stmt])
